sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Everton have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 3 wins and 2 losses, indicating inconsistency. Arsenal, on the other hand, have been more stable, securing 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, demonstrating strong attacking play with goals in every game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters between Everton and Arsenal, the matches ended in draws (1-1 and 0-0), suggesting tightly contested battles. Arsenal have historically dominated with no losses in the last 5 meetings against Everton, winning 3 and drawing 2.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Arsenal sit 2nd in the league with 36 points, boasting a strong offense (30 goals scored) and the best defense (10 conceded). Everton, positioned 10th with 24 points, have struggled offensively (18 goals scored, 18th in the league) but are decent defensively (19 conceded, 7th in least goals conceded).

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Arsenal's Bukayo Saka has been a standout against Everton historically (7.58 average rating, 1 goal, 2 assists in last 5 H2H). Everton are missing key midfielders like Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall and Iliman Ndiaye, which could impact their creativity, while Arsenal miss defender Gabriel Magalhães, potentially weakening their backline.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Arsenal are likely to dominate possession (62% average in last 5 matches) and create more chances, especially in the final third. Everton may rely on a defensive setup and counter-attacks, as seen in their recent wins, but their low big chance conversion rate (30%, 20th in league) could hinder them.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Arsenal's superior form, league position, and historical edge over Everton, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore's 53% probability for an away victory. Everton's home advantage and recent resilience might not be enough to overcome Arsenal's attacking depth and consistency.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Arsenal lead Everton 1-0 at Hill Dickinson Stadium in Liverpool. The sole goal came from a penalty converted by Viktor Gyökeres in the 27th minute, giving Arsenal a deserved advantage. Arsenal have dominated possession and created more threatening opportunities, while Everton have struggled to generate any significant attacking threat. The Toffees will need a drastic improvement in the second half to turn this game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Arsenal have controlled the game with a clear edge in ball possession and more entries into the final third. Their ability to penetrate Everton’s penalty area and create big chances has been evident, culminating in the penalty that led to their goal. Everton, on the other hand, have shown resilience in defensive duels, particularly in the air, but their lack of shots on target and failure to create big chances highlight a significant gap in attacking output. Discipline is also a concern for Everton, with two yellow cards already picked up compared to none for Arsenal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Arsenal showing early intent, consistently building pressure in the opening minutes. Everton managed to wrestle some momentum back around the middle of the first half with a few promising attacking spells, particularly between the 12th and 17th minutes. However, Arsenal regained control soon after, with a sustained period of dominance leading up to the penalty goal. Towards the end of the half, Arsenal continued to push forward with several attacking waves, while Everton struggled to respond with any meaningful threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and impact
halftimeSections.text: For Arsenal, Viktor Gyökeres has been pivotal, confidently dispatching the penalty to give his side the lead. Martin Ødegaard has also been influential with key passes in the attacking third, orchestrating play effectively. On Everton’s side, the lack of attacking contributions is stark, with no player managing a shot on target. Defensively, players like Michael Keane and James Tarkowski have made crucial clearances and interceptions to keep the scoreline from worsening, but they’ve been unable to spark any counter-attacking threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Arsenal are likely to continue their dominance unless Everton can find a way to disrupt their rhythm. The Gunners will aim to capitalize on their possession and create more scoring opportunities, potentially extending their lead. Everton must focus on tightening their defense further while finding ways to transition quickly into attack. They’ll need to take risks to get back into the game, which could open up spaces for Arsenal to exploit. A tactical shift or substitution might be necessary for Everton to inject some energy and creativity into their play.
